prompts.recording_focus:
WHAT TO RECORD (SELECTIVE MODE)
--------------------------------
Only record strategic moments that would be painful to rediscover:
- Scope decisions that changed the product direction
- Demand evidence that validated or invalidated a hypothesis
- Eureka moments — first-principles insights that reframe the problem
- Major shipping events (launched to real users, not just merged a PR)
- Quality gate FAILURES that blocked shipping or changed the plan

Skip the incremental stuff:
- Quality gates that just passed cleanly (record only when they find something)
- Design choices that follow established patterns
- Routine architectural decisions matching existing system style
- User insights that confirm what was already known

✅ GOOD (would be painful to rediscover):
- "Killed the dashboard — users check Slack, not dashboards. Ship insights to them."
- "CEO review expanded scope after finding 3/5 users manually do X every morning"
- "QA found SSE reconnect race condition — the fix changes the reconnect architecture"

❌ SKIP (obvious from context):
- "Chose React for the frontend" (duh)
- "QA passed, all tests green" (expected)
- "Design review approved the color palette" (incremental)
prompts.skip_guidance:
WHEN TO SKIP (BE VERY LIBERAL)
-------------------------------
Skip everything except pivotal moments:
- All routine operations, file reads, git work
- GStack skill runs that produce expected outcomes
- Quality gates that pass without findings
- Design choices following established system
- Incremental user insights confirming known hypotheses
- Any observation where the title would be boring

**If it wouldn't make Garry sit up in his chair, skip it.**
**When in doubt, skip it. Only record what would change someone's approach.**
